narrative: Dear Alex, I know this rejection letter stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away feels like a door slamming shut just as you're reaching for the handle. Our prediction model looked at your profile and estimated a 44% chance you'd struggle to pass the bar exam, which is why we had to make this tough call to protect the program's success rate. What stood out most was your Year 3 decile of 2, placing you near the bottom of your class academically during that critical later stage, which was the biggest signal pulling toward this outcome. Right behind that was your Year 1 decile of 2, showing a pattern of lower performance from the start that echoed concerns about sustaining the grind of law school. Your LSAT score of 25 added to the picture, suggesting the intense analytical demands ahead might prove challenging. Coming from a middle-income family, while admirable in its own right, statistically aligns with added hurdles in this high-stakes path. As an Asian male going full-time, these details layered on, with your undergrad GPA of 2.4 reinforcing a storyline of steady but not standout academic momentum. Together, they painted a composite of someone who's determined but facing an uphill battle against the odds that have tripped up others like you before. It's not about your worth or potential—plenty pivot from here—but this specific blend tipped the scales for us. If you boost those deciles or LSAT through targeted prep, doors could reopen elsewhere. Hang in there; your story isn't over yet.
